• COMPINFO (Insolvency CIRP): Executive Summary: Compuage Infocom Ltd is currently undergoing a Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) initiated by an NCLT order in April 2024, with its affairs managed by an appointed Resolution Professional. The announcement of the 26th Committee of Creditors meeting highlights the company's ongoing severe financial distress and court-supervised bankruptcy proceedings. This constitutes a major red flag for investors, indicating significant risk to equity value and potential for substantial losses as the company navigates insolvency. └ 📄 Source

• INDIACEM (Management Change): Executive Summary: India Cements Limited has disclosed the resignation of Mr. Ashok Ramchandran, a Non-Executive Director, effective June 23, 2026. His departure is attributed to a significant increase in his personal responsibilities and commitments, with the director explicitly confirming no other material reasons influenced his decision. This appears to be a routine board change with no immediate material financial or operational impact, and no apparent red flags for the company. └ 📄 Source

• VIKRAMSOLR (Insolvency CIRP): Executive Summary: Vikram Solar Limited has officially entered Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP), following an NCLT order dated June 12, 2026, triggered by a creditor's petition. An Interim Resolution Professional has been appointed and has initiated the public announcement for inviting claims from creditors. This development represents a material adverse event, indicating severe financial distress for the company, further complicated by an appeal filed by a suspended director against the NCLT order. └ 📄 Source
